findings.evidence: `rg -n "integral basis|abs_discr_le_of_basis_isIntegral" TauCeti/NumberTheory/EffectiveBounds/Discriminant.lean` shows the title at line 8 and bullet at lines 18-19, while the theorem signature at lines 41-43 has only `Module.Basis ι ℚ K` plus `hb : ∀ i, IsIntegral ℤ (b i)`.
findings.fix: Replace “integral basis” in the module documentation with wording like “a rational basis of algebraic integers” or “a `ℚ`-basis consisting of algebraic integers”.
findings.issue: The module title and main-results bullet describe the theorem as a bound from an “integral basis”, which conventionally means a `ℤ`-basis of `𝒪_K`; the theorem only assumes `b : Module.Basis ι ℚ K` with `∀ i, IsIntegral ℤ (b i)`.

summary: The public theorem docstrings are present, but the discriminant module documentation repeatedly uses the standard term “integral basis” for a weaker hypothesis: a `ℚ`-basis whose elements are integral. That is misleading for the advertised main result.
